Q: How many times do you read a chapter?
How often do you engage in taking notes while reading?
Do you make an outline of the content before writing?
How well do you paraphrase the chapter in your own words?
How precise do you consider yourself in delivering the key points of the chapter as they are in your own words?
Options: May mess up in details but deliver the main message

Q: How good are you at distinguishing the main ideas from details?
Options: Most of the times fail to do so
Constantly fail to understand the main points

Q: How do you get along with difficult vocabulary while reading?
Options: Check the meaning from the external sources
Try to understand from the context and sometimes check from external sources
Try to understand from the context and maybe look up the meaning later
Try to understand from the context
Dismiss it, considering it as an unnecessary detail

Q: How often do you compare your summary with the original work?
How often do you preview the title, headings, and first lines of the chapter before going for the actual reading?
Do you highlight or underline the key information or ideas while reading?
How often do you form questions for each heading and sub-heading that you have read in the chapter?
How often do you survey introducing and concluding paragraphs before actually reading the chapter?
How do you deal with the situation when you don't understand the content of the chapter?
Options: Reread and review, even do some external research if it is needed
Review the chapter again to gain some more insights
Briefly review the chapter, without sticking to it
Writing whatever I think about it, without reread the chapter

Q: How confident do you feel that you can explain the chapter after reading it?
How well do you link the content of the previous chapters with the one you have read?
